action_items.task: Conduct a home visit at 442 Grove Street to verify the living situation and meet Michael
action_items.context: Verification of client's residence and meeting his brother/co-habitant.

action_items.task: Maintain a written income ledger logging the dates worked, tasks performed, and payment received
action_items.context: Client is self-employed doing independent landscaping and does not receive standard paystubs.

action_items.task: Bring the income ledger to the next office visit
action_items.context: Required for the PO to verify the client's employment and income.

action_items.task: Do not operate a motor vehicle without a valid state driver's license
action_items.context: Client voluntarily surrendered his license but continues to drive his truck, putting him at high risk of arrest and a parole violation.

critical_updates.details: Client confirmed he is currently domiciled at 442 Grove Street. He continues to reside there with his brother, Michael, in a stable living situation.
Client is self-employed, performing independent landscaping and property maintenance. He exchanges his labor for compensation and will begin tracking this in an income ledger.

Client signed his intake paperwork 'under duress' while citing 'UCC 1-308' to reserve common law rights. He also voluntarily surrendered his state driver's license last month but intends to continue driving his truck (referred to as a 'personal conveyance'), putting him at imminent risk of police contact and a supervision violation.
Client continues to live with his brother, Michael. Client states they tolerate each other but have fundamental differences in approach regarding discipline and structure.

Client exhibits deeply entrenched sovereign citizen/freeman-on-the-land ideology. He actively rejects corporate and state authority, preferring to abide by what he terms 'natural law,' which creates significant friction with standard parole compliance.

next_episode_context: Client David resides at 442 Grove Street with his brother Michael and is self-employed in independent landscaping. He exhibits strong sovereign citizen ideologies, having signed his intake paperwork under duress (UCC 1-308). Crucially, David surrendered his state driver's license last month but continues to drive his truck based on his belief in his 'right to travel,' putting him at high risk for a traffic stop and parole violation. The PO warned him of the consequences and is scheduled to conduct a home visit next week, followed by an office appointment on Tuesday the 14th at 10:00 AM to review his income ledger.
